untranslatable_reason: This signal requires multi-leg cross-market arbitrage detection (synthetic vs futures pricing, correlation analysis across multiple markets, bid-ask spread comparisons, and transaction cost ratio calculations). These require: (1) multi-instrument data feeds not representable in single-instrument OHLCV format, (2) synthetic price construction (which requires option market data and Greeks), (3) cross-market correlation metrics not in the allowed indicator set, (4) relative spread analysis between two separate instruments, and (5) transaction cost modeling. The allowed indicators are single-instrument technical indicators insufficient for implementing arbitrage detection logic.
translation_notes: NATENBERG_DRY_151_0 describes a sophisticated arbitrage strategy requiring multi-leg position monitoring, cross-market comparison, and relative valuation analysis. Standard OHLCV-based technical indicators cannot capture the required synthetic pricing, futures mispricing, correlation thresholds, or spread dynamics. A proper implementation would require a dedicated arbitrage engine with live options chain data, futures quotes, and multi-market surveillance capabilities outside the scope of this backtest framework.
